title: Use buildah repo rather than podman repo
body:
The Podman repo changed and removed a subdir that this tests was relying on.  Rather then relying on a different repo, it would be better to stick to buildah repo for github tests.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,rhatdan,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> LGTM Let's hope nobody tries to remove `tests/bud/from-strach` in the future.

Then lets add a empty file call `do-not-delete` to the directory and put a line in it saying it is used in the `bud-github-context-with-branch-subdir-commit` test. This should raise enough eyebrows when someone tries to remove it.
